# Offboarding {#sec-offboarding}

When you leave the lab — whether moving on to a new position, graduating, or completing a contract — please work through the following steps to ensure a smooth transition.

## Knowledge transfer

- [ ] Document the current state of any projects you are involved in. Write up what is done, what is in progress, and what remains.
- [ ] Make sure all code is committed, pushed, and working on the main branch of the relevant GitHub repositories. Include a clear README.
- [ ] Update or create documentation for any pipelines, scripts, or tools that only you know how to run.
- [ ] Schedule a handoff meeting with the person taking over your work (or with Tamma / the lab manager if no successor is identified yet).

## Data and files

- [ ] Move any important results from scratch storage to the project directory on Savio (scratch is purged after inactivity).
- [ ] Ensure shared datasets you created or maintained are in the correct location with a README (see @sec-project-storage).
- [ ] Remove personal files from shared storage. Clean up your home directory on Savio.
- [ ] Transfer any files from your local machine that the lab needs to Google Drive or the project directory.

## Accounts and access

- [ ] Return any university-issued equipment (laptop, monitor, keys, Cal 1 Card).
- [ ] Your BRC/Savio access will be deactivated when your campus affiliation ends. Make sure nothing critical depends solely on your account.
- [ ] Leave Slack channels gracefully — post a farewell in **#general** if you like.
- [ ] Update your contact information with the lab so we can stay in touch.

## Exit conversation

Before your last day, schedule a short conversation with Tamma to discuss:

- What went well during your time in the lab
- What could be improved
- How the lab can support you going forward (letters of recommendation, references, future collaboration)

We want every lab member to leave feeling good about their experience and well positioned for what comes next.
